// Printing terminal: System.err.println / System.err.printf carrying the caught
// makes it visible. Arg-flow is required (a static message swallows), and only
// System.err is the pinned terminal - System.out is not recognized.
class PrintStderr {
    void printlnCaughtToStderr() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            System.err.println(e);
        }
    }

    void printfCaughtToStderr() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            System.err.printf("failed: %s%n", e);
        }
    }

    void stderrWithoutTheCaught() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001"
            System.err.println("failed");
        }
    }

    void stdoutIsNotTheTerminal() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001"
            System.out.println(e);
        }
    }

    void risky() throws Exception {}
}
